title: Polymarket — Calibration (Phase C)
description: Historical view of Claude's probability calibration on resolved Polymarket markets. The interactive analysis lives in scripts/calibration_analysis.ipynb — this dashboard is a summary surface for ops triage.

options.content:
The calibration harness (Phase C) scores Claude's probability estimates against **resolved** Polymarket markets. It answers the only question that matters before we ever go live: **is there actually edge here?**

## How to run

```bash
# Dry run — prints cost estimate, doesn't call Claude
bash scripts/calibrate_polymarket.sh --dry-run --limit 5

# Small validation run
bash scripts/calibrate_polymarket.sh --limit 25

# Full run (180d, 500 markets)
bash scripts/calibrate_polymarket.sh --window-days 180 --limit 500
```

Results write to `services/polymarket/data/calibration.db`. Open `scripts/calibration_analysis.ipynb` to compute Brier scores, reliability diagrams, and edge-conditional accuracy.

## Decision criteria

| Outcome | Criteria |
|---|---|
| **Green** | Claude Brier < market Brier by ≥ 0.01 on N ≥ 200; \|edge\| ≥ 5% accuracy ≥ 58%; reliability diagonal within 10pt/decile |
| **Yellow** | One criterion fails, others pass. Iterate prompt / threshold. |
| **Red** | Claude Brier ≥ market Brier, OR edge accuracy ≤ 52%. Kill this prompt/model. |

The go/no-go on ever flipping `prediction_markets.enabled: true` is gated on a **green** verdict here — not on Phase A or B completion.

Claude may have memorized the outcome of any market that resolved before its training cutoff. Running calibration on those markets would give an artificially high Brier score — the model isn't reasoning, it's remembering.

The harness enforces a hard filter: **only markets with `resolution_date > cutoff + buffer_days`** (from `config/claude_cutoffs.yaml`) are considered. Today that's `claude-sonnet-4-6` = 2025-01-31 + 30d buffer.

**Second constraint**: the prompt is reconstructed to show only data available at `T = resolution_date - lag_hours` (default 24h pre-close). No final price, no post-resolution news. See `calibrate.py::_build_calibration_prompt`.

## Inspecting the dataset

```bash
docker compose exec polymarket sqlite3 /app/data/calibration.db \
  "SELECT run_id, COUNT(*), MIN(resolution_date), MAX(resolution_date) FROM calibration_runs GROUP BY run_id"

docker compose exec polymarket sqlite3 /app/data/calibration.db \
  "SELECT claude_model, AVG((claude_prob - resolution)*(claude_prob - resolution)) AS claude_brier, \
          AVG((market_prob_at_T - resolution)*(market_prob_at_T - resolution)) AS market_brier, COUNT(*) \
   FROM calibration_runs GROUP BY claude_model"
```

Any row in `calibration_runs` with `resolution_date <= cutoff` is a bug — file it and re-run. The harness should reject those at fetch time.

panels.title: Total calibrated markets
panels.description: Row count in calibration_runs. At N < 200 the Brier comparison is not conclusive.

targets.rawQueryText: SELECT COUNT(*) AS n FROM calibration_runs
targets.queryText: SELECT COUNT(*) AS n FROM calibration_runs

panels.title: Claude Brier (lower is better)
panels.description: Mean squared error of claude_prob vs. resolution across all rows. Market Brier is the benchmark — Claude must beat it by ≥ 0.01 for a green verdict.

targets.rawQueryText: SELECT AVG((claude_prob - resolution)*(claude_prob - resolution)) AS claude_brier FROM calibration_runs WHERE claude_prob IS NOT NULL AND resolution IS NOT NULL
targets.queryText: SELECT AVG((claude_prob - resolution)*(claude_prob - resolution)) AS claude_brier FROM calibration_runs WHERE claude_prob IS NOT NULL AND resolution IS NOT NULL

targets.rawQueryText: SELECT AVG((market_prob_at_T - resolution)*(market_prob_at_T - resolution)) AS market_brier FROM calibration_runs WHERE market_prob_at_T IS NOT NULL AND resolution IS NOT NULL
targets.queryText: SELECT AVG((market_prob_at_T - resolution)*(market_prob_at_T - resolution)) AS market_brier FROM calibration_runs WHERE market_prob_at_T IS NOT NULL AND resolution IS NOT NULL

panels.title: Brier delta (market − claude)
panels.description: Positive = Claude beats the market. Green threshold is +0.01 per decision criteria.

targets.rawQueryText: SELECT AVG((market_prob_at_T - resolution)*(market_prob_at_T - resolution)) - AVG((claude_prob - resolution)*(claude_prob - resolution)) AS brier_delta FROM calibration_runs WHERE claude_prob IS NOT NULL AND market_prob_at_T IS NOT NULL AND resolution IS NOT NULL
targets.queryText: SELECT AVG((market_prob_at_T - resolution)*(market_prob_at_T - resolution)) - AVG((claude_prob - resolution)*(claude_prob - resolution)) AS brier_delta FROM calibration_runs WHERE claude_prob IS NOT NULL AND market_prob_at_T IS NOT NULL AND resolution IS NOT NULL

panels.title: Per-model Brier breakdown
panels.description: Brier scores sliced by claude_model. If you run multiple models, this is where you compare them head-to-head.

targets.rawQueryText: SELECT claude_model, COUNT(*) AS n, ROUND(AVG((claude_prob - resolution)*(claude_prob - resolution)), 4) AS claude_brier, ROUND(AVG((market_prob_at_T - resolution)*(market_prob_at_T - resolution)), 4) AS market_brier, ROUND(AVG((market_prob_at_T - resolution)*(market_prob_at_T - resolution)) - AVG((claude_prob - resolution)*(claude_prob - resolution)), 4) AS brier_delta FROM calibration_runs WHERE claude_prob IS NOT NULL AND market_prob_at_T IS NOT NULL AND resolution IS NOT NULL GROUP BY claude_model ORDER BY n DESC
targets.queryText: SELECT claude_model, COUNT(*) AS n, ROUND(AVG((claude_prob - resolution)*(claude_prob - resolution)), 4) AS claude_brier, ROUND(AVG((market_prob_at_T - resolution)*(market_prob_at_T - resolution)), 4) AS market_brier, ROUND(AVG((market_prob_at_T - resolution)*(market_prob_at_T - resolution)) - AVG((claude_prob - resolution)*(claude_prob - resolution)), 4) AS brier_delta FROM calibration_runs WHERE claude_prob IS NOT NULL AND market_prob_at_T IS NOT NULL AND resolution IS NOT NULL GROUP BY claude_model ORDER BY n DESC

panels.title: Edge-conditional accuracy
panels.description: For rows where |claude_prob - market_prob_at_T| ≥ the bucket threshold, how often did Claude's side win? This is the metric that matters for the live strategy.

targets.rawQueryText: WITH bucketed AS (SELECT CASE WHEN ABS(claude_prob - market_prob_at_T) >= 0.10 THEN '>=10%' WHEN ABS(claude_prob - market_prob_at_T) >= 0.05 THEN '5-10%' WHEN ABS(claude_prob - market_prob_at_T) >= 0.02 THEN '2-5%' ELSE '<2%' END AS edge_bucket, CASE WHEN (claude_prob > market_prob_at_T AND resolution = 1) OR (claude_prob < market_prob_at_T AND resolution = 0) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS claude_wins FROM calibration_runs WHERE claude_prob IS NOT NULL AND market_prob_at_T IS NOT NULL AND resolution IS NOT NULL) SELECT edge_bucket, COUNT(*) AS n, ROUND(AVG(claude_wins), 3) AS accuracy FROM bucketed GROUP BY edge_bucket ORDER BY CASE edge_bucket WHEN '>=10%' THEN 1 WHEN '5-10%' THEN 2 WHEN '2-5%' THEN 3 ELSE 4 END
targets.queryText: WITH bucketed AS (SELECT CASE WHEN ABS(claude_prob - market_prob_at_T) >= 0.10 THEN '>=10%' WHEN ABS(claude_prob - market_prob_at_T) >= 0.05 THEN '5-10%' WHEN ABS(claude_prob - market_prob_at_T) >= 0.02 THEN '2-5%' ELSE '<2%' END AS edge_bucket, CASE WHEN (claude_prob > market_prob_at_T AND resolution = 1) OR (claude_prob < market_prob_at_T AND resolution = 0) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS claude_wins FROM calibration_runs WHERE claude_prob IS NOT NULL AND market_prob_at_T IS NOT NULL AND resolution IS NOT NULL) SELECT edge_bucket, COUNT(*) AS n, ROUND(AVG(claude_wins), 3) AS accuracy FROM bucketed GROUP BY edge_bucket ORDER BY CASE edge_bucket WHEN '>=10%' THEN 1 WHEN '5-10%' THEN 2 WHEN '2-5%' THEN 3 ELSE 4 END

panels.title: Reliability diagram (decile bins)
panels.description: For each predicted-probability bin, compare Claude's mean prediction to the realized frequency of resolution=1. Perfect calibration is a diagonal: bin midpoint ≈ realized.

targets.rawQueryText: SELECT CAST(MIN(10, CAST(claude_prob * 10 AS INTEGER)) AS INTEGER) AS decile, COUNT(*) AS n, ROUND(AVG(claude_prob), 3) AS mean_pred, ROUND(AVG(resolution), 3) AS realized_freq, ROUND(AVG(resolution) - AVG(claude_prob), 3) AS diagonal_gap FROM calibration_runs WHERE claude_prob IS NOT NULL AND resolution IS NOT NULL GROUP BY decile ORDER BY decile
targets.queryText: SELECT CAST(MIN(10, CAST(claude_prob * 10 AS INTEGER)) AS INTEGER) AS decile, COUNT(*) AS n, ROUND(AVG(claude_prob), 3) AS mean_pred, ROUND(AVG(resolution), 3) AS realized_freq, ROUND(AVG(resolution) - AVG(claude_prob), 3) AS diagonal_gap FROM calibration_runs WHERE claude_prob IS NOT NULL AND resolution IS NOT NULL GROUP BY decile ORDER BY decile

panels.title: Recent calibration rows
panels.description: Most recent 50 market evaluations. Spot-check prompt quality and obvious misreads.

targets.rawQueryText: SELECT resolution_date, SUBSTR(question, 1, 80) AS question, category, ROUND(claude_prob, 3) AS claude_prob, ROUND(market_prob_at_T, 3) AS market_prob_at_T, resolution, claude_model FROM calibration_runs ORDER BY resolution_date DESC LIMIT 50
targets.queryText: SELECT resolution_date, SUBSTR(question, 1, 80) AS question, category, ROUND(claude_prob, 3) AS claude_prob, ROUND(market_prob_at_T, 3) AS market_prob_at_T, resolution, claude_model FROM calibration_runs ORDER BY resolution_date DESC LIMIT 50

options.content:
Phase C is manual by design — each run costs Anthropic API calls and should be deliberate. To add a weekly rolling refresh:

```bash
# Weekly, Sunday 06:00 UTC
0 6 * * 0 cd /path/to/crypto-portfolio && bash scripts/calibrate_polymarket.sh --limit 200 --window-days 30 >> logs/calibration.log 2>&1
```

The panels above pull directly from `calibration.db`, so a new run shows up in Grafana without any additional plumbing.

After each full calibration run, drop a short write-up in `docs/polymarket_calibration_YYYY-MM-DD.md` with:

1. **N and date range** of the run (e.g. 450 markets resolving 2025-03-01 → 2025-09-01)
2. **Brier delta** (market - claude) with 95% CI
3. **Edge-conditional accuracy** at \|edge\| ≥ 5%
4. **Verdict** (green / yellow / red) per the criteria above
5. **Reliability diagram screenshot** from the notebook
6. **Decision** — what the next change is (prompt tweak, threshold shift, or kill)

This is a research log, not release notes. Keep it honest; yellow is the most likely outcome on the first run and that's fine.
